who.title: Who are we?
who.description: Hyperloop UPV is a team of 50 students from multiple disciplines from the Universitat Politècnica de València united with the objective of designing, implementing and perfecting the hyperloop reception.

mission.title: Mission
mission.description: Motivate young talents to train and innovate in a multidisciplinary environment through the development of hyperloop technology that transforms mobility in a sustainable and responsible way.

vision.title: Vision
vision.description: To be a leading international university team, recognized for leading innovation in future transport and generating solutions on a global scale.

commitment.title: Commitment
commitment.description: Loyalty, responsibility and awareness with team, university and partners

innovation.title: Innovation
innovation.description: Creativity and pioneering spirit to overcome technological limits and explore new ideas

learning.title: Learning
learning.description: Continuous development of young talent and multidisciplinary growth.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.description: Positive and responsible impact with the development of hyperloop technology

ehw.title: European Hyperloop Week
ehw.description: In 2019, we decided to embark on a challenging report alongside 3 of the best European universities (University of Edinburgh, Delft University of Technology, ETH Zurich) hyperloop technology event where they could share the best of our ideas and forge a community fueled by the vision of making hyperloop reality.
ehw.conclusion: Thus was born the European Hyperloop Week (EHW), an event that goes far beyond a mere competition, driven by the insatiable curiosity of young students and the desire to learn.

competition.title: Hyperloop Pod Competition
competition.description: In 2015 a team of 5 students from the Universitat Politècnica de València decided to take on the challenge presented by SpaceX and change the world of global hyperloop competition. This first year, a conceptual proposal was presented and the university team traveled to California to experience the competition and ended up winning multiple awards.
competition.conclusion: From the resounding success of what we now know as 'HI - THE FUTURE CONCEPT' each year the experience of developing a hyperloop model has been carried out to present it at international competitions and continue working to develop the transportation of the future.

trajectory.description: Discover the milestones that define our journey.

investigation.description: Discover the milestones that define our journey.

why.title: Why hyperloop?
why.description: Hyperloop presents itself as an alternative to other means of transport because it combines high speed, energy efficiency and less environmental impact. Unlike other conventional means, it reduces travel times without depending on fossil fuels.

how.title: How does it work?
how.description: Hyperloop is a high-speed transport system that combines magnetic levitation, electric propulsion and low-pressure environments. Its operation is based on three elements:

vacuum.title: Vacuum tube
vacuum.description: By reducing the amount of air, aerodynamic resistance is minimized, which is the main speed limit in conventional transport systems.

capsule.title: Pod or capsule
capsule.description: Passengers or cargo travel inside pressurized pods. These pods are designed to be light, aerodynamic and safe.

levitation.title: Levitation and propulsion
levitation.description: The pod moves along the track thanks to magnetic levitation systems, eliminating friction. Acceleration is achieved through electric linear motors distributed along the track.
